claim: The manuscript's herbal section uses a combination of substitution and transposition ciphers, which would explain the higher entropy levels compared to other sections.
evidence_for: Higher entropy in the herbal section (3.8478 bits) compared to other sections
Glyph positional constraints may reflect cipher structure rather than grammar
test: Compare word and glyph entropies between the herbal and other sections using a two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test

tests_run.test: compare word and glyph entropies between the herbal and other sections using a two-sample kolmogorov
tests_run.details: Section entropy std dev: 0.0593. Range: 3.7149 - 3.9016. Variable across sections.

reasoning: If confirmed, this would suggest that the cipher is more complex in the herbal section, narrowing the solution space
